definition: The condition, state, or quality of being free or as free as possible from all flaws or defects; excellence.
example: We will pursue perfection in every aspect of the game: drop a pass, you run a mile—no excuses.

definition: The state of being united or joined as a whole; harmony or agreement.
example: Without unity on this hallowed ground, we too will be destroyed, just as they were.

definition: A feeling of deep admiration for someone or something elicited by their abilities, qualities, or achievements; due regard.
example: I don't care if you like each other or not, but you will respect each other.

definition: The action of leading a group of people or an organization; the state or position of being a leader.
A settled way of thinking or feeling about someone or something, typically one that is reflected in a person's behavior.
The combined action of a group, especially when effective and efficient.
example: Teamwork shouts from both sides of the field: left side, strong side—one team, one purpose.

definition: A person or thing that wins something; a person who is successful or victorious.
example: I'm a winner. I'm going to win.

definition: Greatly revered or respected; sacred; made holy.
example: If we don't come together right now on this hallowed ground...
We will not be perfect until we play as one—together is the only formation that wins.

definition: Having all required elements; free from flaw in effort and standard.
example: We will be perfect in every aspect of the game—you drop a pass, you run a mile.

definition: A call of unity and strength from one half of a divided team.
example: Left-side stands at the center of the argument: Left side! Strong side!—the chant that turned two sides into one body.

definition: To give attention with the ear; to heed.
example: You will listen to each other, or you will listen to the sound of losing.

definition: A team sport; here, a vehicle for character and integration.
example: Football is the classroom; character is the exam you cannot cheat.

definition: A firmly held belief or opinion.
example: Conviction without evidence is noise; conviction after struggle is leadership.

definition: The quality of being coherent and intelligible.
example: Clarity is kindness in speech—confusion is often a way to hide.

definition: The quality of being honest and having strong moral principles.
example: Integrity is what you do when the speech ends and no one is applauding.
